hypothesis: Within pure period lengths 1 through 10 and preperiod lengths 0 through 3 with period lengths 1 through 5, every nonzero trace reconstructs a nonzero initial-left cell by depth 500.
interpretation: Every description in both finite boxes was checked exactly. The only survivors are redundant descriptions of the constant-zero trace, and none satisfy the required c_0=1 seed condition. The deepest first failure was 11 for pure periods and 10 for the bounded eventual-period box. These finite exclusions do not address unbounded preperiod, period, or witness depth.
limitations: Pure periods longer than 10 are untested.
The eventual-period search does not cover preperiods above 3 or periods above 5.
Candidate counts are description counts and retain equivalent redundant words.
The fixed-width graph state bound grows exponentially with retained width.
No finite search proves eventual nonperiodicity.
proof_scope: Exact exhaustive enumeration of 2046 pure-period descriptions and 930 bounded preperiod-plus-period descriptions through left depth 500, plus 14 exact width-four functional graphs.
question: problem1
